channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Piles of Rubbish and Bottles, 10th July
item.description:
There is a huge pile of rubbish that has been accumulating over the last few months and is constantly being added to. There are bottles and rubbish dumped opposite the Plumstead Health Centre. This is now attracting rodents and unpleasant for staff who have to park here that work in the Centres
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Strandfield Close, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped Sofa, 10th July
item.description:
There is a dumped sofa outside Bettswood Court on Anerley Rd.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Goffs Lane Low Tree Branches, 10th July
item.description:
I would like to report a problem with low-hanging tree branches and overgrown bushes along the Goffs Lane in our bus operation area. Issue already has been raised with SCC and informed via I bus Team. The vegetation has become overgrown and is extending into the roadway, creating difficulties for buses and other large vehicles passing through. The low branches and bushes are restricting access, reducing visibility, and may pose a safety risk to drivers, passengers, and other road users. I would be grateful if the council could arrange for an inspection and carry out any necessary pruning or maintenance work as soon as possible to ensure the road remains safe and accessible. Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to hearing from you regarding any action that will be taken.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 311, Goff's Lane, Broxbourne
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Persistent Fly tipping problem, 10th July
item.description:
This alley is being targeted for fly tipping and excess of rubbish for months. Today it had furniture and pieces of wood, cardboard and packaging materials over the floor. The bins have excess of rubbish with open lids that smell and allow animals to climb into them. Finally the bins are leaking fetid liquids that stink and attract insects.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pound Place, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Toyota Hiace AM08 RNU, 10th July
item.description:
Car has been here for months now, not moved, multiple PCNs, no tax, no MOT and flat tyre. This is a serious safety hazard for the residents of Wistow House and needs to be removed immediately.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whiston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corner Palmerston’s road, 10th July
item.description:
This tree has gotten so big that everyone has to duck under it or walk into the sidewalk as you can see the bottom of it is lower than most cars so everyone was routinely walking into the street a child is going to get hit by a car by avoiding this tree many children walk down this pavement please can you fix this tree

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 54b;54c;56;58, Hemstal Road, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping on the corner of Floyd Road and Charlton Church Lane, 10th July
item.description:
This corner of Floyd Road and Charlton Church Lane is a continuous issue of overflowing bins and fly tipping. It is digsuting. Especially as this corner of the road is the first thing visitors see when walking up the hill from Charlton Train station. It doesn't create a very good first impression.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Church Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter Bins Fill, 10th July
item.description:
It is Friday and the litter bins are full again. If they are not emptied today they will be overflowing by Saturday morning.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thicket Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees obscuring light, 10th July
item.description:
Most of the lampposts down this street are obscured by the foliage from trees. This means the street and pavements are very dark at night which is a danger to pedestrians who could trip on the uneven pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11, Woodbine Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 220 Edington Road: children’s play equipment at side of recycling bin., 10th July
item.description:
Camera might be a good deterrent.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Edington Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Side of 170 Edington Road: huge cardboard box., 10th July
item.description:
Box has been thrown on to raisedbed at side of 170 Edington Road.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Edington Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Side of 73 Edington Road: black trolley on wheels., 10th July
item.description:
Fly-tip is near concrete bollards on grass verge at side of 73 Edington Road.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Edington Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins left out, 10th July
item.description:
Some bins have been left out on the street outside 77 Lassell Street, and I think foxes have been at two of them.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lassell Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree overhanging, can't walk along pavement, 10th July
item.description:
Tree overhanging, can't walk along pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 144, Brook Drive, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked pavement, 10th July
item.description:
Several complaints have been made regarding pavements being blocked by traders using public pavements as parts of their business premises. For over the past 2 weeks Enfield Food Centre 602-4 Hertford Road have been blocking the pavement with large crates of produce, piles of rubbish and shopping baskets. This is not the first time complaints have been lodged for the same reason. It would appear that the traders have little or no regard for the council officers or the public. Are businesses now permitted to trade on the public footpaths without paying for the space in their business rates?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hertford Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Side of 75 Felixstowe Road: white shelf unit, shoes and wood and metal object., 10th July
Rubbish all along the street, you said it was resolved and done nothing!, 10th July
item.description:
Rubbish all along the street, bottle, plastic, waste. Don't just close the ticket, actually sort it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 97, Brook Drive, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sprayed TP, 10th July
item.description:
TP sprayed need serious cleaning

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southwark Bridge Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Side of 96 Felixstowe Road: fridge., 10th July
item.description:
Fridge is on grass verge that leads to Mottisfont Road.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Luffield Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cycleway bollards removed, 10th July
item.description:
Once again in less than 24 hours of the bollards being replaced, they have been removed by One Stop Builders Merchants to facilitate deliveries to their premises The cost of continually replacing the bollards to the council and in turn to the residents via their council tax is unacceptable. To keep replacing the bollards only to have them removed immediately is not a solution Large Lorrie’s delivery to One Stop on a daily basis. They park in the cycle lane and on the pavements. Disruption to traffic and pedestrians is constant. A fixed permanent static bollard needs to be installed here to stop the constant removal of the bollards because they interfere with the business of One Stop. It is also noted that along Enfield Highway other businesses are now also removing the bollards outside of their premises where they are deemed to impede in their businesses

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hertford Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet
